title: minuartia howellii
intro: Minuartia howellii, also known as Howell's catchfly, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Caryophyllaceae family and is native to the western United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent effects, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Minuartia howellii include the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues, as well as its potential use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Herbal preparations of Minuartia howellii include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by drying and processing the plant's leaves, stems, and roots.
